titleStmt.title: Letter from Joseph E. Devlin to the Urban District Council, Longford, 26 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Joseph E. Devlin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Joseph E. Devlin, the assistant secretary for the Local Government Board in Dublin Castle, to the Longford Urban District Council regarding the Education (Provision of Meals (Ireland)) Act 1916 which will see a raise in the rating limit.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Local for Board. 26th May. 1916
Education (Provision of Meals (Ireland) Acts Sir â I am directed by the Local for Board
for Ireland to forward, for the information of your
Council the enclosed copy of the Education,
(Provision of Meals (Ireland) Act 1916, which
recently the Royal Royal Assent. It will be observed that the effect of this Act
is to raise the rating limit for the purposes of the
Premium Act of 1914 from one halfpenny to one penny
limiting the duration of that statute has been
repealed. I am Sir, your obd servt J E Devlin Asstt Secty; The Clerk Of EachUrban District
